methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $33M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QunaSys is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Tennin Yan that has raised $33M to date. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, among them IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Q-CTRL.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the narrow quantum computing cohort—only 1 company matched Series B criteria, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum needed for reliable triangulation. Stage-progression analysis, however, is applicable. Using $33M in total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series B to subsequent-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $63M.

Given the constraint of a single applicable method, the triangulated range stands at $53M (low), $63M (median), and $72M (high). The median of $63M represents the most defensible point estimate under current data. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer trading or financing comparables at identical stage and the quantum computing sector's early-stage heterogeneity, which limits the reliability of step-up assumptions. A fuller peer-based analysis would require either tighter stage matching or acceptance of broader sector comparables with acknowledged reduced precision.
